fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: The task is to open Notepad. On the current screen, I do not see Notepad open. To open Notepad, I should click on the Start menu (Windows icon) to search for and launch Notepad.

After clicking the Start menu (Settings icon), the Start menu is now open. To open Notepad, I should use the search bar at the top of the Start menu to search for 'Notepad'. The search bar is identified by the text 'Search for apps; settings; and documents'. I should click on this search bar to begin typing.
The Start menu search bar is now active and ready for input. To proceed with opening Notepad, I should type 'Notepad' into the search bar so that I can find and launch the application.

The search for 'Notepad' has been entered in the Start menu search bar. On the current screen, I see the Notepad++ application (Box ID: 1) listed, but Notepad++ is not the default Windows Notepad app. The user requested to open Notepad, not Notepad++. I do not see the default Notepad app in the visible results. To check if it is further down the list, I should scroll down to see more search results.
